howItWorks.intro: This pension calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How are pensions calculated?
faq.answer: Most pensions use: Final Average Salary × Years of Service × Multiplier. A common multiplier is 1.5-2.5%. With 25 years at 2%, you get 50% of your final salary.

faq.question: Lump sum or monthly payments?
faq.answer: Monthly payments provide guaranteed lifetime income. Lump sums give you control but require disciplined investing. Lump sum value is typically 12-18× the annual benefit.

faq.question: Are pensions taxable?
faq.answer: Yes, pension payments are generally taxed as ordinary income at the federal level. Some states exempt pension income from state taxes.

faq.question: What if my company goes bankrupt?
faq.answer: Private pensions are insured by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation (PBGC) up to about $6,750/month for age 65 retirees. Government pensions have separate protections.

howWeCalculate.intro: This pension calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.

whenToUse.intro: This pension calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.

When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
When you want to verify a quote or estimate from a financial advisor or lender
